_meta.description: Semantic-opposite pairings for structural_inversions (SI_NN). Used by pipeline.operators.mental_models.invert() to flip a candidate's structural_inversion to its inverse. Pairs are symmetric: when (A,B) is listed, invert(A) -> B AND invert(B) -> A. Unlisted SIs produce no mutant on the structural_inversion axis.
_meta.policy: Partial coverage is a feature: only pairings with a clearly defensible semantic opposite are listed. Adding speculative pairs would dilute the operator's signal.

rationale.SI_01<->SI_22: Protagonist IS the institution (defends system) <-> villain's cause is righteous (system is the wrong target).
rationale.SI_02<->SI_12: No villain, harm from policy <-> most dangerous person is the one trying hardest to help.
rationale.SI_03<->SI_13: Hero caused the harm before story begins <-> hero answered the call and the call was wrong.
rationale.SI_04<->SI_11: Antagonist + protagonist share identical goal <-> two stories that appear separate are the same story.
rationale.SI_05<->SI_18: Investigation becomes confession (turn inward) <-> journey to save the world becomes finding one person.
rationale.SI_06<->SI_15: Competence is the problem (the big act fails) <-> the small act is what changes everything.
rationale.SI_07<->SI_09: Ending is neither victory nor defeat (no closure) <-> success reveals failure (closure inverts).
rationale.SI_08<->SI_17: Hero changes world by refusing to change self <-> power comes from letting go (changing self).
rationale.SI_14<->SI_19: World has no villain or solution (entropy) <-> love is the irrational choice that creates meaning.
rationale.SI_16<->SI_21: Monster was protecting them (threat is care) <-> unknown world is familiar (alien is home).
rationale.SI_20<->SI_23: Child teaches the adult (wisdom from below) <-> ordinary person in extraordinary world (extraordinariness from within).
